idiom: a leopard can't change its spots
meaning: used for saying that someone will never change their behaviour or character
a weak feature of someone or something that could cause failure or be attacked
an event such as a flood or earthquake produced by natural forces that people have no control over

idiom: be born with a silver spoon in your mouth
meaning: to have advantages because you come from a rich family
to change a situation by doing something very different from what most people usually do
to gain control or advantage over a person or situation

idiom: give sb a taste/dose of their own medicine
meaning: to treat someone in the same bad way they treat other people
if success goes to your head it makes you think you are better than you really are
to accept a difficult situation without complaining
to be able to think quickly and make sensible decisions
in an area far from the town or city

idiom: it's as broad as it's long
meaning: used for saying that you cannot choose between two things because they are equal

meaning: to tell people exactly what you are thinking or intending to do

to use your influence in order to get something you want

meaning: to punish someone in your own way without involving the police or courts
to punish someone very severely
